_doc: Compact protocol/schema dictionary. Use this for quick lookup of wire schemas, object schemas, and current proof status; use SESSION_FINDINGS for long notes and flow_model.json for logic/control-flow.
_updated: 2026-06-20 s11 charselect milestone; master client zlib-sync create RPC decoded
_rules: Every complex protocol/schema finding should add or update an entry here.
Each entry should have shape/status/evidence/next, not long prose.
Line refs or search tags should point back to SESSION_FINDINGS and Ghidra output.

gss.shape: TCP bytes: client 4-byte XOR window header + XOR(lower packet); no zlib

master.shape: client 4-byte XOR header; server 9c 01 1c 02; XOR(zlib_frame(concat lower packets))

lower_head.shape: u32 body_len | u8 0 | u16 opcode | 9 zero bytes | body

master_client_zsync.shape: XOR(clear type-4 frame) where each clear frame is u32 raw_len | u32 compressed_len | deflate chunk; compressed chunks continue one zlib stream with sync-flush tails, so standalone zlib.decompress per chunk is wrong.
master_client_zsync.next: Use ZlibSyncFrameDecoder for client->server master logs before parsing create-character replies/requests.

0x4009_client_signin.shape: Authenticator_WebSessionKey body from FTLauncher args: token Code, local session key, version/path/provider

0x4002_client_challenge_response.shape: u32 challenge response body under lower head

0x4005_client_channel_signin.shape: u32 + u32 + u32 body after channel click

0x1006_server_signin_result.shape: u8 success + u32 master_id + u32 channel_id + string host + string port
